title: acalypha hassleriana var. glandulosa
intro: Acalypha hassleriana var. glandulosa is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as a type of spiderwort or acalypha. The medicinal properties of Acalypha hassleriana var. glandulosa have been traditionally used in various cultures to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Acalypha hassleriana var. glandulosa is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and roots can be used to make tinctures or salves, offering a versatile range of herbal preparations.
